figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic illustration of symmetry operations and their effects on ground states. (a) In a magnetic system with n-fold degenerate ground states, the switching symmetry gνµS maps the ground state ψµ to ψν , thereby transforming the order parameter from Oµ to Oν . In contrast, the magnetic space group symmetry gµM leaves ψµ and Oµ invariant. (b) Left: In a FM with a single sublattice, the identity E preserves the ground state, whereas the time-reversal T reverses the net magnetization M = m1. Right: In an AFM with two sublattices, the Néel vector N = (m1 − m2)/2 is reversed under either T or the translation t. The four-fold rotation C4 rotates both M (FM) and N (AFM) by 90◦.

FIG. 3. Switching cases in FMs and AlMs. (a),(b) Schematic diagrams of net magnetization (M ∥ ±ẑ) reversal in a FM driven by (a) a magnetic field H ∥ ±ẑ or (b) a charge current JC ∥ ∓ẑ passing through a fixed FM layer with magnetization Mfix ∥ +ẑ, where H and Mfix are slightly tilted to initiate the dynamical process. (c) Switching symmetries (C2,[100] and C2,[010]) for a single magnetic moment m1 in an orthorhombic cell. (d),(e) Atomistic simulations of M reversal between M± ∥ ±[001] driven by (d) H ∥ ±[001] and (e) a current-induced polarization p ∥ ±[001], each with a slight tilt. (f),(g) Schematic diagrams of (f) field-free and (g) field-assisted switching of Néel vector (N ∥ ±x̂) in AlM CrSb films (top layers), achieved by JC ∥ ±x̂ passing through the bottom heavy metal layers. (h) Switching symmetries (C2,[100] and C2,[001]t1/2, where t1/2 is a half-translation along [001]) for a CrSb thin film with m1 and m2. (i),(j) Atomistic simulations of N reversal between N± ∥ ±[001] with DM vector d ∥ [100] in (i) field-free (p ∥ ∓[120]) and (j) field-assisted (p ∥ ±[100] and H ∥ [001]) modes.
